old_claim.note: Naval Logbooks provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: The Depot was a state military archive conserving maps, plans, memoirs, printed works, and manuscript works, so archival practice enables the institution; naval voyage logs are outside scope.

source_shape.source_locator: Arabesques article says the Depot general de la Guerre conserved military-domain documents, including institutional archives and printed and manuscript works.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source presents the Depot as an archive and document-conservation institution for the military domain.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports public archives as the enabling institutional practice and rejects naval logbooks, which are a different maritime record type outside the corrected scope.

ontology_before: Military Map Rooms depended directly on Naval Logbooks, mixing maritime voyage records into a land-war cartography and archive office.
ontology_after: Depot de la Guerre Military Map Archive depends on public_archives for state document conservation and archival practice.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: military_map_rooms no longer depends on naval_logbooks and now depends on public_archives.
would_reject_if: The node were rescoped to naval chart rooms or shipboard logbook systems rather than the Depot de la Guerre.
A source showed the Depot's map archive depended specifically on naval logbook practice.
The public_archives node were narrowed away from state archival repositories.
short_reason: The corrected node is an archive office; naval logbooks are outside scope.
